All committed content is on the record. 692 693 - **On background:** Vault-safe but identity-protected. Use carefully — this is a public repo. 694 695 - **Off the record:** Ephemeral. They have distinct, non-overlapping responsibilities. 710 711 | Layer | Obsidian Sync | Git / GitHub | 712 | --- | --- | --- | 713 | Vault content (`.md` files) | Syncs across devices | Tracked | 714 | Plugin code (`main.js`, `manifest.json`) | Desktop only (see below) | Tracked — public record | 715 | Plugin settings (`data.json`) | Desktop only, E2E encrypted | **Gitignored** — never reaches GitHub | 716 | `community-plugins.json` | Per-device (not synced) | Tracked — canonical desktop plugin list | 717 | Workspace state (`workspace.json`, `graph.json`) | Per-device | Gitignored — machine-local noise | 718 719 **Rule:** Obsidian Sync is the private courier for credentials and machine state. Git is the public record for vault content and plugin presence. The desktop processes them. 729 730 ### Obsidian Sync Settings — Desktop 731 732 | Toggle | Setting | 733 | --- | --- | 734 | Core settings | ON | 735 | Appearance | ON | 736 | Hotkeys | ON | 737 | Active core plugins | ON | 738 | Active community plugins | ON | 739 | Installed community plugins | ON | 740 741 ### Obsidian Sync Settings — Mobile (Pixel) 742 743 | Toggle | Setting | 744 | --- | --- | 745 | Core settings | ON | 746 | Appearance | ON | 747 | Hotkeys | ON | 748 | Active core plugins | OFF — phone does not need slides, audio-recorder, webviewer, etc. | 749 | Active community plugins | OFF — decouples plugin lists; desktop keeps 26, phone keeps 0 | 750 | Installed community plugins | OFF — phone does not need 140 plugin directories | 751 752 ### Why Per-Device Plugin Lists 753 754 Obsidian Sync defaults to pushing the same plugin list to all devices. On a 20K-file vault, the desktop's 26-plugin stack (Dataview indexing, Omnisearch search index, Linter watching saves, MCP tools looking for a REST API server) overwhelms mobile. Disabling community plugin sync on the phone lets each device run its own appropriate stack while content (`.md` files), appearance, and hotkeys still flow both ways. 755 756 ### Conflict Duplicates 757 758 When both devices edit the same config file between syncs, Obsidian creates a `(2)` copy (e.g., `app (2).json`). These are already gitignored via `.obsidian/**(2)*.json`. Delete them when found — Obsidian only reads the original filename. 759 760 761 ## Git Practices 762 763 764 765 - Branch naming: 766 767 - `claude/description-sessionId` for Claude Code branches 768 769 - `copilot/description` for GitHub Copilot branches 770 771 - `gemini/description` for Gemini agent branches 772 773 - Commit messages: Clear, descriptive, explain the "why" 774 775 - Never force-push without explicit permission 776 777 - Check in before anything irreversible 778 779 - The legislature scraper workflow commits directly to main for automated bill updates 780 781 782 783 --- 784 785 786 787 788 ## Character Set & Notation 789 790 Emojis are **first-class vault syntax** as of 2026-04-02 (Logan, superseding prior ASCII-only directive). 791 792 - Authority: [Unicode Consortium](https://unicode.org) â€” the canonical source for emoji definitions, codepoints, and names (see [full emoji list](https://unicode.org/emoji/charts/full-emoji-list.html)) 793 - Emojis may appear in note titles, frontmatter, body text, DOCKET entries, and commit messages 794 - Use semantically â€” emojis carry meaning and should reinforce, not decorate 795 796 --- 797 ## Guiding Principles 798 799 - The five W's: who, what, when, where, why 800 - The four C's: collect, capture, catalogue, collate 801 - Public repo = on the record 802 - Markdown for human product. Update it when you start or finish work. 854 855 856 857 Task assignment flows through GitHub Issues (with `agent:*` labels) and Linear (SWARM label). Slack carries breadcrumbs. The vault is the record. 858 859 860 861 --- 862 863 864 865 ## Runtime Portability Standard (MESHWEB) 866 867 Cross-environment runtime portability — cloud instance vs. local CLI vs. GitHub Actions CI. Defines which capabilities are available in each environment, substitution conventions for gaps, and the MESHWEB Registry of env-scoped artifacts. 868 869 See `MESHWEB.md` for the full standard. 870 871 --- 872 873 ## Portable Path Standard (NETWEB) 874 875 876 877 The vault must work identically on **any platform** — Windows (NTFS), macOS (APFS/HFS+), Linux (ext4), iOS/Android (Obsidian mobile), and CI runners (GitHub Actions). Both NTFS and APFS are **case-insensitive**; only Linux is case-sensitive. This standard targets the **lowest common denominator** of all target filesystems. 878 879 880 881 ### Forbidden filenames (any extension, any case) 882 883 `AUX`, `CON`, `NUL`, `PRN`, `COM0`–`COM9`, `LPT0`–`LPT9` 884 885 These are Windows reserved device names inherited from MS-DOS.
